_comment: Grade-level orientation for Ignatius Press's 'Faith and Life' catechetical series (grades 1-8), widely used by Catholic Classical homeschoolers alongside Ambleside Online's living-books catalog. Sourced from Ignatius Press's own public product pages and scope-and-sequence materials (loc.ignatius.com/faithandlife) — book titles are exact; 'topics' are broad thematic threads, NOT a claimed-exhaustive chapter-by-chapter table of contents (different sources disagree on exact chapter counts per printing/edition, so an invented precise numbering would be more likely to mislead than help). This is deliberately the same kind of thing the Ambleside Online catalog already stores for other subjects: metadata and curator context, never the book's actual copyrighted text. A parent should still set SessionConfig.current_unit for chapter-exact precision matching their own copy of the book — Bede favors that over this file whenever it's set (see services/ai_service.py's _get_catalog_context).
series: Faith and Life (Ignatius Press, Third Edition)

1.theme: Creation, the fall, and God's promise of a Savior — a first, wonder-filled introduction to salvation history.
topics: God our Creator, and our home in Heaven lost through original sin
The world prepares for the Savior
The Incarnation and the life, death, and Resurrection of Jesus
The Holy Spirit, the Church, and Mary our Mother

2.theme: Preparation for First Confession and First Communion, with God's law and salvation history as background.
topics: The Blessed Trinity and God our Creator
God's law, and obeying God our Father through love
The Savior promised, born, and revealed — the Holy Family, Jesus our Teacher
Sin, forgiveness, and the Sacrament of Penance
The Last Supper, the Passion, and the Resurrection
The Holy Mass

3.theme: Salvation history from Creation through Abraham and Moses, God's law, and deepening participation in the Mass.
topics: God loves us; God created the world
The promise of a Savior; Abraham, father of God's people
The prophet Moses and God's laws
Loving God most of all; purity and truth
Offering Jesus to the Father; the Bread of Life (the Eucharist)

4.theme: The Old Testament story of salvation history in fuller depth, and the Ten Commandments as "road signs" toward Heaven.
topics: The Fall, and turning away from God (Cain and Abel)
Israel's story: Joseph, the Exodus, and the Promised Land
The Kings of Israel — David, Solomon, and the promise of a new King
The Ten Commandments as the way to Heaven
Jesus, Our Guide, and growing in love

5.theme: A fuller treatment of the Creed, article by article — what Catholics believe, and why.
topics: The Apostles' Creed, article by article
The Blessed Trinity, creation, and the fall
The life, death, and Resurrection of Christ
The Church, the Communion of Saints, and the Last Things

6.theme: The Ten Commandments in depth, the Mass, and the Last Things, with a focus on living a genuinely Christian life.
topics: The Ten Commandments explored in depth
The Mass and the sacraments as sources of grace for Christian living
Virtue and moral decision-making
Death, judgment, Heaven, and Hell — the Last Things

7.theme: Grace and virtue, and the interplay of faith and reason, building on the doctrinal overview of earlier grades.
topics: Sanctifying and actual grace
The theological and cardinal virtues
Faith and reason
Sin, conversion, and growth in holiness

8.theme: The Church's history, structure, and mission — the Mystical Body of Christ — and preparation for lived adult Catholic life.
topics: The Church's history and structure
The Church as the Mystical Body of Christ
The sacraments of service (Holy Orders and Matrimony)
Christian life and the Last Things
